examples.title: Left Recursion Elimination
examples.description: Algorithm to eliminate left recursion from a CFG
examples.explanation: This algorithm identifies productions where non-terminal appears as first symbol (direct left recursion), then transforms them to right-recursive form using a new auxiliary non-terminal.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × m) where n = non-terminals, m = productions

examples.title: Left Factoring Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to perform left factoring on CFG productions
examples.explanation: Left factoring extracts common prefixes from productions with the same LHS, creating a new non-terminal to handle the remaining suffixes. Essential for predictive parsing.

examples.title: CYK Parsing Algorithm
examples.description: CYK algorithm for parsing strings using CFG in CNF
examples.explanation: CYK uses dynamic programming to check if a string can be derived by the grammar. Time complexity O(n³ × |P|) where n is string length and |P| is number of productions.

examples.title: CFG to PDA Conversion
examples.description: Algorithm to convert CFG to equivalent Pushdown Automaton
examples.explanation: This algorithm constructs a PDA that simulates leftmost derivations by using the stack. Non-terminals are pushed onto stack and replaced according to productions. Terminals are matched and popped from stack.
